attributes.description:
Sets the text color of login error messages (messages that appear when login fails or validation errors occur). This determines what color the error message text appears in, typically a contrasting color to ensure visibility against the error background.
CSS variable: --wm-login-error-text-color

Sets the background color of login error messages (messages that appear when login fails or validation errors occur). This is the main background color that appears behind the error message text.
CSS variable: --wm-login-error-background-color

Sets the color of the border around login error messages. This determines the color of the outline that defines the error message container.
CSS variable: --wm-login-error-border-color

Controls the corner rounding of login error messages. This makes the error messages appear with rounded corners for a softer appearance.
CSS variable: --wm-login-error-border-radius

Controls the top padding (vertical spacing) inside the login form container. This creates breathing room above the form content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-login-form-padding-top

Controls the bottom padding (vertical spacing) inside the login form container. This creates breathing room below the form content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-login-form-padding-bottom

Controls the left padding (horizontal spacing) inside the login form container. This creates breathing room on the left side of the form content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-login-form-padding-left

Controls the right padding (horizontal spacing) inside the login form container. This creates breathing room on the right side of the form content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-login-form-padding-right
